tips.title: Tips & Explanation
dashboard: Revenue is the sum of total_revenue from all transactions in the selected period.
Margin % = (Revenue - COGS) / Revenue × 100. Fill in COGS in Settings → Menu for accuracy.
Peak Hour = hours where revenue is above the 75th percentile.
Margin Leakages = items with margin below target (40%). Prioritize high-volume items.
margin: Gross Profit = Total Revenue - Total COGS (Cost of Goods Sold).
Healthy margin target for a cafe: at least 40%. Below 25% needs immediate action.
Top leakages = items with low margin AND significant sales volume (≥1% of total revenue).
Suggested price is calculated to reach 40% margin: Suggested Price = COGS ÷ (1 - 0.4).
If COGS is not filled, margin shows 0. Complete it in Settings → Menu.
menu_performance: Stars: High revenue & high margin → keep and promote widely.
Cash Cows: High revenue but low margin → improve COGS efficiency or slightly raise price.
Question Marks: Good margin but rarely ordered → promote more actively, try during peak hours.
Dogs: Low revenue & low margin → evaluate, reposition, or remove from menu.
Matrix uses median revenue and median margin as quadrant thresholds.
peak_hours: Golden Hours = hours with revenue above the 75th percentile of all hours.
Dead Hours = hours with revenue below the 25th percentile (only hours with transactions).
Golden Hours strategy: upsell, bundle packages, extra staff, full stock.
Dead Hours strategy: flash promos, happy hour, 2x loyalty points, special lunch menu.
Hour data requires the 'hour' or 'Time' column from your transaction data.
basket: Basket Analysis uses receipt numbers to group items per transaction.
Support = how often an item pair appears across all transactions (0-100%).
Confidence A→B = of all transactions with item A, what % also has item B.
Anchor = items frequently bundled with others (high popularity & support).
Companion = frequently bundled but less popular alone — great for cross-selling.
Bundle recommendations use price = total individual - 10% discount.
forecast: Forecast uses simple linear regression on daily revenue data.
R² (R-Squared) measures model accuracy: closer to 1.0 means better prediction.
More historical data (minimum 14 days) makes forecasts more accurate.
Use forecasts for stock planning, staffing, and sales targets.
Confidence interval (grey band) shows the range of likely actual revenue.
discounts: Positive discount ROI means discounts increase average transaction value.
Compare avg revenue with vs without discounts to measure effectiveness.
Frequent discounts during peak hours (golden hours) can reduce profitability.
Use strategic discounts during dead hours to increase traffic in slow periods.
anomalies: Anomalies are detected using the IQR (Interquartile Range) method.
Revenue outlier = transaction with value outside normal bounds (Q1-1.5×IQR or Q3+1.5×IQR).
Volume spike = hour with far more transactions than normal — may signal a special event.
Investigate negative anomalies (very low revenue) to find potential fraud or data entry errors.
comparison: Comparison compares the current period vs the previous period (same length).
Positive growth (green) = better performance than the previous period.
Use monthly comparison to evaluate the effectiveness of promotional strategies.
customers: Customer insights are based on purchase pattern grouping per receipt.
Single-item vs multi-item transactions show customer purchase depth.
Average basket value increases significantly for 3+ item purchases — encourage with bundling.
payments: Favorite payment methods help allocate POS terminals and negotiate MDR fees with banks.
QRIS and digital wallets usually have lower MDR than credit cards.
Dominance of one payment method can be a risk — diversify payment options.
inventory: Inventory forecast uses average quantity sold per day × forecast horizon.
Safety stock is calculated from daily sales variability.
High-variance items need a larger buffer stock to avoid stockouts.
promo_simulator: The simulation calculates the impact of discounts on revenue and profit based on historical data.
Volume boost = assumed sales increase from the promotion (estimated, needs field validation).
The optimal discount is one that increases total profit (not just revenue).
Check the 'Profit After Promo' column to ensure the promo is not losing money.
menu_optimizer: Menu optimizer combines margin, volume, and trend analysis to give recommendations.
Prioritize items with high scores across all dimensions (revenue, margin, growth).
Items with low scores on all dimensions need evaluation — remove or reformulate.
staff: Staff data requires the 'collected_by' column from your POS system (available in Moka).
Staff ranking is based on total revenue processed, not hours worked.
Use this data for performance evaluation and team rewards/incentives.
kpi: KPI targets are set based on cafe industry standards and your historical data.
Achievement above 100% means the target was exceeded — update the target for the next period.
Action plans help connect data insights to concrete team actions.
